# Spec: <title>

Status: Draft
Owner: <owner>
Created: <yyyy-mm-dd>
Linked proposal: <path-or-none>
Linked ADR: <path-or-none>
Linked plan: <path-or-none>
Support-tier impact: <none|surface and intended tier change>
Policy impact: <none|ledger or rule affected>

## Required behavior

State the behavior that must be true. Prefer concrete inputs, outputs, files,
commands, and failure modes.

## Acceptance examples

List examples, fixtures, or scenarios that demonstrate the required behavior.

## Failure modes

List cases that must fail closed, produce explicit diagnostics, or avoid silent
success.

## Required evidence

List proof commands and artifacts. Do not claim stability without a command that
can prove the claim.

## Implementation ownership

Name the components, files, or subsystems expected to carry the behavior.

## Non-goals

List related behaviors that are intentionally out of scope.

## Claim boundary

State exactly what users, reviewers, or agents may claim after the required
evidence passes.

## Rollback

Describe the smallest safe rollback or disablement path.
